message:
feat(rpc): add graceful Ctrl+C shutdown for RPC mode

RPC/stdin mode previously had no signal handling — a Ctrl+C during an active
RPC session would terminate the process immediately without running the
main_impl shutdown flush (session persistence, cleanup). This could leave
session JSONL files in an inconsistent state.

Changes:
- Install a ctrlc handler that triggers an AbortHandle
- Race the RPC stdio future against the abort signal using futures::select
- When the signal fires, return Ok(()) to let main_impl's existing shutdown
  path (session flush, extension cleanup) run normally
- If the ctrlc handler fails to install (e.g., another handler exists),
  log a warning and proceed without signal handling (graceful degradation)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
